definition: The Kalecki profit identity links AI capex (~$250B) to aggregate S&P 500 earnings as the marginal growth driver, though the fiscal deficit ($1.8-2T) is 10-15x larger and provides a floor. A 25% capex cut (~$60-75B) is only 3-4% of the fiscal deficit floor. The directional channel is real but magnitude is more modest than initially framed.
evidence: AI capex ~$250B sustains growth at the margin
25% AI capex cut (~$60-75B) is 3-4% of fiscal deficit floor
Accounting identity is irrefutable; magnitude was moderated in debate
open_questions: Whether fiscal floor erodes if consolidation occurs simultaneously
Second-order effects through employment and supplier networks
